body.text: Combined USDT + USDC market cap now ~$260B, roughly 3x its 2023 level, as the July 18 GENIUS Act compliance deadline approaches.
GENIUS Act final rules due July 18

Six US regulators (OCC, FDIC, NCUA, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC) must finalize stablecoin frameworks by July 18, 2026, one year after the GENIUS Act was signed. Rules include a $5M issuer capital floor and no deposit insurance for token holders.
1:1 reserve and licensing mandate tightens the field
Every issuer must hold 1:1 reserves in cash or short-term Treasuries and submit to audits. Circle and Coinbase can absorb compliance costs; smaller issuers may be forced to exit or partner up.
Banks moving into the $323B stablecoin market
Traditional banks are entering stablecoin issuance and tokenized deposits as the market matures, competing directly with Circle's USDC and Tether's USDT for payment rails share.
Accounted for over 40% of June's total crypto losses. Attackers moved stolen funds across Bitcoin, Solana, Hyperliquid, and BNB Chain to obscure the trail.
Second-largest incident of the month, continuing the pattern of cross-chain bridges as a top attack surface for DeFi protocols.
Roughly 40% of cumulative 2026 losses trace back to stolen or compromised private keys rather than smart contract bugs, per PeckShield's monthly tally.
